trigger: React i18n library selection for 47 existing pages with zero-latency English bundle requirement
chosen: react-i18next + i18next-http-backend with bundle-first loading (English inlined via main.tsx resources, non-English lazy-loaded from /api/translations/:ns/:lng)
rationale: react-i18next's native fallbackLng mechanism makes G6 fallback free — zero custom fallback code needed in components. Namespace == module slug matches existing pages/ directory. Backend plugin supports lazy loading with cache-bust param per deploy.

trigger: Non-English translation requests must minimize AI cost and latency
chosen: 5-step pipeline: tenant cache → policy gate → marketplace probe → marketplace-delta AI translation → full tenant translation. Marketplace delta-empty path issues zero new AI calls.
rejected: Always translate all keys via AI on every miss (no marketplace cache)
rationale: Marketplace modules share base translations across tenants. Delta translation only covers tenant-specific additions. Cache-first + marketplace-delta reduces AI spend ~80% vs naive per-tenant translation.

chosen: FREEDOM (tenant-configurable via PUT /tenants/:id/config). MACHINE_LOCKED_KEYS stays at 4 keys (tenantId, masterTenantId, provisionedAt, subscriptionTier).
rationale: Tenant admins need to change default locale without code deploy. MACHINE keys are compile-time invariants (tenant identity) — i18n preferences are business config, not architectural invariants.

trigger: How does TranslationRequestRegistrar learn a user's HTTP Accept-Language at registration time?
chosen: Extend the existing AccountCreated event payload with optional acceptLanguage?: string field. Formalized via AccountCreatedPayload interface in engine-contracts/user-registration-contracts.ts (cross-flow contract).
rationale: One event per business fact. Adding an optional field is a backwards-compatible extension; introducing a second event would force every consumer of registration to subscribe to two events. Cross-flow contract typed interface unblocks BFA validation.

trigger: Which AI keys translate marketplace base terms — tenant's or master's?
chosen: MarketplaceTranslationCacheJob (T667) runs under MASTER_TENANT_ID CLS context and uses master AI keys. Tenant-specific delta translations run under ALS tenant context using tenant AI keys. CF-811 iron rule: ONLY T667 may switch CLS context for translation.
rejected: Use master keys for every translation (cheaper but violates tenant cost attribution)
rationale: Tenant-specific translation cost attributes to tenant. Marketplace base terms are shared infrastructure cost, attributable to master. CLS context switch is a well-tested bootstrap pattern (bootstrap-seeder.service.ts).

trigger: What HTTP status does GET /api/translations return on AI failure / policy denial / CAS failure?
chosen: HTTP 200 { fallback: true, locale: 'en' } in ALL error paths. No 4xx or 5xx from this endpoint. i18next's native fallbackLng handles the UI side.
rejected: 500 on AI failure, 403 on policy denial
rationale: User never sees broken UI from translation failures. i18next's fallbackLng expects a successful response envelope. Making the contract monotonic 200 simplifies client code and removes a class of bugs (error-boundary propagation).

trigger: When tenant admin changes i18n.defaultLocale, does it affect users who explicitly picked a locale?
chosen: userOverride: true field on user-preferences. Once true, tenant defaultLocale changes do NOT propagate. Only the user can reset by explicitly selecting a new locale (still userOverride: true) or by pressing 'Reset to tenant default' (userOverride: false).
rejected: Tenant default always wins (simpler but violates user consent)
rationale: Users who chose a locale expect that choice to persist across tenant config changes. Tenant admin changing defaultLocale should affect users who never chose (userOverride false), not users who did (userOverride true).

trigger: How to prevent accidental cross-tenant translation ref writes?
chosen: CF-810: every F1523.storeRef() call uses tenantId = ALS.tenantId at write time. No parameter override. MarketplaceTranslationCacheJob (T667) is the only exception — it runs under MASTER CLS so its ALS.tenantId == MASTER_TENANT_ID, still following the rule.
rationale: DNA-5 scope isolation pattern: fabric methods read TenantContext internally, never accept tenantId as param. Consistent with IDatabaseService.searchDocuments(). Makes BFA scope_isolation arbiter enforceable — any parameter override fails CF-810.
